my_thoughts: The reversal works: objective consciousness = practical (state effect), philosophical = idea (our conception). The student attempted mapping (attempting to map labels), which shows they understand: objective consciousness = philosophical (classification). They'll likely accept the reversal. The episode is complete: categorization → multi-scale → mapping → reversal. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Two-turn pattern: T1: Student asked categorization (categorizing practice)—multi-scale refusal: self-remembering = multiple scales. T2: Student attempted mapping ('Objective consciousness seems to belong to the philosophical scale?')—attempting to map labels via offers classification and seeks confirmation. They understand: objective consciousness = philosophical (classification). The comparison_contrast mechanism reverses: objective consciousness = practical (state effect), philosophical = idea (our conception) ('Quite the reverse: it is very practical. But if you mean for us, then, certainly, objective consciousness is a philosophical idea'). Pattern complete: categorization question → multi-scale refusal → mapping attempt → reversal. They've shifted: objective consciousness = practical (state), philosophical = idea (conception).
visible_response:
Quite the reverse: it is very practical. But if you mean for us, then,
certainly, objective consciousness is a philosophical idea.
At the same
time the study of descriptions of glimpses of this state is possible.
If one
studies these descriptions and tries to find similarities, it can become
theoretical.
